refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operational parameters, and optimizing energy efficiency provides a clearer decomposition aligned with system capabilities and stakeholder needs.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through energy consumption telemetry data, control command logs, and performance reports demonstrating energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over the evaluation period.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operational parameters to optimize energy use.
FR3: Provide feedback and alerts to operators and managers on energy efficiency status.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy and load sensors with telemetry system for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms in vehicle control system to optimize energy use.
DP3: Develop dashboard and alerting services within Safety Services and Teleoperation modules for feedback.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and feedback aspects of energy efficiency.

potential_sources: Telemetry data shared between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Control commands from Teleoperation to Vehicle
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via known interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). The design maintains modular separation with clear interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable sensor data for energy and tonnage measurements.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting vehicle safety and performance.
Dependency on network link reliability for telemetry and control.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Further detail control algorithm safety validation and fallback modes in next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical vehicle systems including sensors and control actuators responsible for energy monitoring and adaptive control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends real-time energy consumption and tonnage data to Teleoperation and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives control commands from Teleoperation module to adjust operational parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone enabling data exchange between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as sensors and control logic are physically and logically within Vehicle.
Receives telemetry data and sends commands but does not contain core control logic.
Primarily for alerting and feedback, minimal containment of control functions.
Overall system boundary, not a direct implementation module.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy monitoring sensors and adaptive control algorithms are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and maintain safety-critical integrity.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module may increase integration and testing effort.
Dependency on network link reliability for telemetry and control commands.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider partitioning control algorithm into submodules if complexity grows; validate network link robustness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched with selected DPs that logically satisfy the functional requirements with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists due to telemetry data crossing module boundaries but is mitigated by clear interface definitions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor Vehicle for core control and monitoring, aligning with safety criticality.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis, control command logs, and operator feedback within assigned modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to Vehicle and supporting modules with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Detail fallback and safety mechanisms for control algorithms in Vehicle.
Strengthen network link reliability requirements and monitoring.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, additional detail on safety validation and fallback modes for control algorithms is needed due to high safety criticality.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1.1: Accurately measure energy consumption with sensor calibration and fault detection.
FR1.2: Accurately measure tonnage moved with load sensors and data fusion.
FR2.1: Adapt vehicle operational parameters based on real-time energy data.
FR2.2: Implement safety fallback modes for control algorithms.
FR3.1: Generate energy efficiency alerts and reports for operators and managers.

revise.why: To incorporate safety validation and fallback mechanisms explicitly in the control algorithm design.

assumptions: Sensor data is reliable and timely.
Network link provides sufficient bandwidth and reliability.
open_risks: Network link failures affecting telemetry and control.
evidence_needed: Sensor calibration and fault detection test results.
